channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: DA-linked lobbying firm faces scrutiny over political connections and client dealings
item.description: Resolve Communications, a lobbying firm chaired by former DA leader Tony Leon, is under intense political scrutiny after allegations surfaced that it leveraged its connections with DA-led governments ...

item.title: Impeachment of Vice President Sara Duterte Faces Political Obstacles
item.description: The article discusses the ongoing impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte, highlighting the political and legal challenges faced. The Supreme Court initially blocked the trial by ruling the H...

item.title: Derryn Hinch: A Controversial Journalist's Legacy in Australian Media
item.description: Derryn Hinch, a prominent Australian journalist known for his fiery personality and unapologetic style, had a career marked by controversy and conviction. His dismissal from Adelaide's 5DN in 1999 hig...

item.title: Ombudsman Remulla Advises Public Officials to Avoid Conflict of Interest Between Business and Politics
item.description: Ombudsman Jesus Crispin Remulla has urged government officials to avoid conflicts of interest by clearly separating their roles in business and politics. During a radio program, Remulla emphasized tha...

item.title: Saturday Racing Tips: Best Bets and Odds from Newmarket and York
item.description: The article provides detailed racing tips and betting advice for Saturday's horse racing events at Newmarket and York. It highlights key races, including the Dubai July Cup Stakes (Group 1) and the Al...

item.title: Zanzibar's ruling party and opposition agree to reconciliation deal
item.description: Citizens and political leaders across Zanzibar have celebrated a landmark reconciliation agreement between the ruling Chama Cha Mapinduzi (CCM) party and opposition party ACT Wazalendo, viewing it as ...

item.title: First Nation secures jurisdiction over child welfare through landmark tripartite agreement
item.description: The Uashat mak Mani-utenam band council has officially taken control of its youth protection services, implementing a community-led legal framework under the Tshisheuatishitau Law. This historic agree...

item.title: Pakistan military continues anti-terror operations in Balochistan, eliminating 88 militants since July 5
item.description: Pakistan's military, Frontier Corps, and police are intensifying operations in Balochistan under 'Operation Shaban' to eliminate terrorists linked to attacks on security forces. The operation, which b...

item.title: Bradley John Murdoch's Final Interview Reveals No New Clues in Peter Falconio Case
item.description: The article details the release of Bradley John Murdoch's final interview with NT Police, conducted less than three weeks before his death. Murdoch, convicted of murdering British backpacker Peter Fal...

item.title: Bihar Government Takes Disciplinary Action Against Over 3,000 Teachers for Fake Degrees
item.description: The Bihar government has announced plans to dismiss more than 3,000 teachers who allegedly obtained fake degrees and forged certificates to secure appointments. Education Minister Mithilesh Tiwari rev...

item.title: Car Crash on Raila Odinga Road Leaves Fire and Casualties
item.description: A vehicle veered off Mbagathi Road in Nairobi and crashed onto a pedestrian footbridge near Riara University at around 6am on Saturday, July 11. Witnesses reported the car drove up the footbridge stai...

item.title: Peter Obi and His Driver Fined for Parking Violation at Abuja Airport
item.description: The Nigerian presidency has responded to claims by former Governor Peter Obi that he was unfairly targeted by authorities after his car was clamped at Nnamdi Azikiwe International Airport. Aviation Mi...

item.title: Governor Commends Tinubu and Security Agencies for Successful Rescue of Oriire Schoolchildren and Teachers
item.description: Governor of Imo State, Senator Hope Uzodimma, praised President Bola Tinubu and Nigeria's security agencies for rescuing 56 schoolchildren and teachers kidnapped in Oriire, Oyo State. The operation, w...

item.title: E-STAR Report Highlights Access Barriers in Early Kidney Transplant Stages
item.description: The 2026 Early Steps to Transplant Access Registry (E-STAR) Annual Data Report by the Regenstrief Institute's Transplant Research for Enhancing Access Team (TREAT) provides critical insights into chal...

item.title: KMTC CEO Confirms Death of Student and Details Ongoing Investigations
item.description: The Kenya Medical Training College (KMTC) has confirmed the death of student Ann Adhiambo Odhiambo, who was found dead in her hostel room at the Nairobi campus. KMTC CEO Dr. Kelly Oluoch addressed the...

item.title: Spain Advances to Semi-Finals After Merino's Late Goal Against Belgium
item.description: Spain secured a hard-fought 2-1 victory over Belgium in a thrilling World Cup match, advancing to the semi-finals. The decisive goal came from midfielder Koke Merino in the 89th minute, sealing a dram...

item.title: Opinion: Delays in Canada's project approval process are slowing promised UAE investment
item.description: In this opinion column, Jesse Kline argues that Prime Minister Mark Carney's efforts to attract major foreign investment are being undermined by Canada's slow regulatory and project approval system. T...

item.title: Tanzania's WHI launches 250 staff housing project in Dar es Salaam
item.description: WATUMISHI Housing Investment (WHI) has announced plans to build over 250 residential units for government employees in Dar es Salaam, addressing the city's growing housing shortage. The project, locat...

item.title: Pakistan Calls for Diplomatic Restraint and Upholding of Islamabad MoU Amid US-Iran Tensions
item.description: Pakistan's UN representative has called on all parties involved in the US-Iran tensions to uphold the Islamabad Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) and exercise restraint to prevent further escalation. ...

item.title: Male fertility decline: Scientific debate over environmental and health factors
item.description: The article explores the controversy surrounding declining male fertility, with scientists divided on the causes. Prof Hagai Levine's research suggests a 50% drop in testosterone levels over 50 years,...
